title: Escalation Matrix and Contact Directory
content:
## Purpose

This procedure defines the escalation paths, contact information, and decision criteria for escalating security incidents to the appropriate personnel. Having a clear, current escalation matrix ensures incidents receive the right level of attention without unnecessary delays or communication gaps.

## Scope

Applies to all SOC analysts and incident responders when an incident requires escalation beyond their authority, expertise, or scope. Covers business-hours escalations, after-hours procedures, and management chain notifications.

## Prerequisites

- Access to the current escalation matrix (this document — maintain as a living document)
- Access to the SOAR platform for automated escalation workflows
- Understanding of incident severity levels (see Severity Classification Matrix)
- SOC collaboration channels accessible from personal devices for after-hours contact

## Procedure

### Escalation Levels

**Level 1 — SOC Analyst Escalation (Tier 1 to Tier 2)**

When to escalate:
- Alert cannot be dispositioned within the 15-minute triage window
- True positive confirmed but requires deeper forensic analysis
- Alert involves a technique or tool the Tier 1 analyst has not been trained on
- Multiple correlated alerts suggest a larger incident

How to escalate:
1. Document your triage findings in the SOAR case — what you investigated, what data sources you checked, and what remains unclear.
2. Assign the case to the Tier 2 on-duty analyst in the SOAR platform.
3. Send a direct message to the Tier 2 analyst with a brief summary and the case link.
4. For urgent items (active threat), call the Tier 2 analyst directly.

**Level 2 — Incident Escalation (Tier 2 to IR Lead / Tier 3)**

When to escalate:
- Incident is confirmed P2 or higher
- Active compromise requiring containment coordination across multiple teams
- Threat actor demonstrates advanced TTPs requiring specialized analysis
- Incident requires forensic imaging, malware reverse engineering, or threat intelligence correlation
- Scope of the incident is expanding despite containment efforts

How to escalate:
1. Update the case with your full analysis, containment actions taken, and remaining unknowns.
2. Assign the case to the IR Lead or Tier 3 specialist.
3. Call the IR Lead directly for P1/P2 incidents — do not rely on email or messaging alone.
4. If the IR Lead is unavailable, escalate to the SOC Manager.

**Level 3 — Management Escalation (IR Lead to SOC Manager / CISO)**

When to escalate:
- P1 incident confirmed — Incident Commander designation required
- Containment action will impact a production business service
- Data breach with regulatory notification implications
- Media attention or public disclosure anticipated
- Law enforcement engagement recommended
- Incident exceeds the IR team's capacity or capability

How to escalate:
1. Call the SOC Manager / CISO directly. Leave a voicemail AND send a text/secure message.
2. Provide a concise briefing: What happened, what is affected, what we have done, what we need.
3. If CISO is unavailable, escalate to the CTO/CIO with a copy to the CISO's delegate.

**Level 4 — Executive Escalation**

When to escalate:
- Material business impact (revenue loss, customer data breach, regulatory fine risk)
- Incident requires board notification
- External communications or press response needed
- Ransom payment consideration
- Incident involves a company officer or executive

How to escalate:
1. CISO briefs the CEO/executive team per the Incident Communication Plan.
2. Provide the Executive Incident Briefing Template (see separate document).
3. Schedule an executive war room if extended coordination is needed.

### Escalation Contact Directory

**Note: This is a template. Replace with actual names, numbers, and emails. Review and update monthly.**

| Role | Primary Contact | Backup Contact | Phone | Email | After-Hours |
|------|----------------|----------------|-------|-------|-------------|
| Tier 2 Analyst (On-Duty) | [Rotation schedule] | [SOC Queue] | [Number] | soc-tier2@company.com | Same |
| IR Lead | [Name] | [Name] | [Number] | ir-lead@company.com | Phone/Signal |
| Tier 3 / Forensics | [Name] | [Name] | [Number] | forensics@company.com | Phone |
| SOC Manager | [Name] | [Name] | [Number] | soc-mgr@company.com | Phone/Signal |
| CISO | [Name] | [Name] | [Number] | ciso@company.com | Phone |
| CTO / CIO | [Name] | [Name] | [Number] | cto@company.com | Phone |
| Legal Counsel | [Name] | [External Firm] | [Number] | legal@company.com | Emergency line |
| Privacy Officer | [Name] | [Backup] | [Number] | privacy@company.com | Phone |
| Communications/PR | [Name] | [Agency] | [Number] | comms@company.com | Phone |
| HR Business Partner | [Name] | [Name] | [Number] | hr-security@company.com | Phone |
| IT Operations Lead | [Name] | [Name] | [Number] | it-ops@company.com | On-call pager |
| Network Operations | [Name] | [Name] | [Number] | noc@company.com | NOC hotline |
| Identity/IAM Team | [Name] | [Name] | [Number] | iam@company.com | On-call |
| Cloud Operations | [Name] | [Name] | [Number] | cloudops@company.com | On-call |
| Cyber Insurance Carrier | [Carrier Name] | [Broker] | [Number] | [Claim email] | 24/7 hotline |
| External IR Firm | [Firm Name] | N/A | [Number] | [Retainer contact] | 24/7 |
| FBI Cyber Division | Local Field Office | IC3 (ic3.gov) | [Number] | N/A | 24/7 |
| CISA | cisa.gov/report | (888) 282-0870 | 888-282-0870 | report@cisa.gov | 24/7 |

### After-Hours Escalation Procedure

1. **For P1 incidents discovered after hours:**
   - Call the IR Lead directly. If no answer within 10 minutes, call the SOC Manager.
   - If neither responds within 20 minutes, call the CISO.
   - Simultaneously, begin containment actions per the relevant playbook — do not wait for management approval if active data destruction or exfiltration is occurring.
   - Send a text/Signal message to all three contacts with: Incident ID, one-line summary, severity, current actions.

2. **For P2 incidents discovered after hours:**
   - Message the IR Lead and SOC Manager via secure channel.
   - If no response within 30 minutes and the situation is worsening, call directly.
   - Begin investigation and containment per playbook.

3. **For P3/P4 incidents discovered after hours:**
   - Document in the SOAR platform and include in the next shift handoff.
   - No after-hours call-out required unless the incident escalates.

### External Escalation Contacts

| Scenario | Contact | When |
|----------|---------|------|
| Ransomware with potential OFAC implications | Legal + external ransomware negotiator | Before any communication with threat actor |
| Data breach in EU (GDPR) | Privacy Officer + DPA contact | Within 72 hours of awareness |
| Data breach with PHI (HIPAA) | Privacy Officer + HHS | Within 60 days; immediately for 500+ |
| Payment card data breach | PCI Forensic Investigator (PFI) + acquiring bank | Within 24 hours |
| Active nation-state threat | CISA + FBI | As directed by CISO and Legal |
| Insider threat — criminal activity | Legal + law enforcement | After Legal assessment |

### Escalation Matrix Maintenance

1. The SOC Manager is responsible for maintaining the escalation matrix.
2. Review and update contact information monthly.
3. Test after-hours contact procedures quarterly (call tree drill).
4. Update immediately when personnel changes occur (new hires, departures, role changes).
5. Distribute the updated matrix to all SOC team members after each update.
6. Store the matrix in the SOAR platform and as a printed copy in the SOC physical space.

## Escalation Criteria

- Contact in the escalation chain is unreachable: Follow the backup contact column; if backup is also unreachable, escalate to the next level up
- Disagreement between teams on incident severity or response approach: SOC Manager or Incident Commander makes the final call
- Vendor or third-party SLA for incident support is not being met: Escalate to vendor account manager and procurement

## References

- NIST SP 800-61 Rev. 2, Section 2.4 — Incident Response Team Structure
- ITIL v4 — Escalation Management
- Organization's Incident Response Plan
- Organization's Business Continuity Plan — Emergency Contact Directory
